title: Controlled tmux terminal sessions for Ananta CLI/TUI, Hub, Workers and HTTPS/OIDC gateway

summary.short: Add tmux-backed terminal sessions to Ananta in a controlled, policy-driven way, with local CLI/TUI support and optional HTTPS/WSS access secured through OIDC and the existing authorization model.
summary.problem: Ananta currently has CLI/TUI and Hub/Worker orchestration, but no first-class controlled terminal session model. Simply exposing tmux or a shell would create a high-risk remote command surface, especially if the Hub itself can be connected in addition to Workers.
summary.goal: Introduce terminal sessions as explicit governed runtime objects. Sessions must be bound to user identity, target type, target id, workspace, goal/task context and authorization decision. Hub terminal access must be possible but denied by default and treated as a separate high-risk permission class.
summary.non_goal: Do not expose raw tmux sockets, raw shell ports, unauthenticated terminals, or implicit Hub shell access through normal Worker permissions. Do not turn admin access into unrestricted root-equivalent terminal access.

context.existing_cli_path: The existing Ananta CLI uses ANANTA_BASE_URL, ANANTA_USER and ANANTA_PASSWORD to authenticate against the Hub and then calls API endpoints with a Bearer token.
context.existing_tui_path: The existing CLI documentation already exposes ananta tui and ananta web as official user commands.
context.existing_hub_worker_mode: The local setup supports ROLE=hub and HUB_CAN_BE_WORKER=true, plus optional separate Workers. This means terminal targeting must distinguish Hub, Worker and Hub-as-Worker explicitly.
context.security_position: Terminal access is a high-impact capability. It must be represented as its own authorization surface, not as a side effect of Goal, Task, Worker or Admin access.

components.name: TerminalSessionService
components.summary: Central service responsible for creating, attaching, listing, writing to, reading from and terminating terminal sessions.
responsibilities: Persist terminal session metadata
Resolve and validate terminal target
Call TerminalPolicyService before every high-risk operation
Delegate process-level work to a backend implementation such as tmux
Emit audit events for lifecycle and I/O operations

components.name: TmuxSessionBackend
components.summary: Backend implementation that manages tmux sessions safely and deterministically.
responsibilities: Create tmux sessions with safe deterministic names
Attach to existing tmux sessions
Send input to tmux panes
Capture output from tmux panes
Kill sessions and clean stale sessions
Prevent shell injection through session names, window names or target arguments

components.name: TerminalPolicyService
components.summary: Authorization layer for terminal-specific permissions and risk classes.
responsibilities: Evaluate whether the user may create, attach, read, write or kill a session
Differentiate terminal.worker.*, terminal.hub.* and terminal.hub_as_worker.* permissions
Enforce default-deny for Hub terminal access
Block unsafe workspace and secret access unless explicitly permitted
Return deterministic allow/deny decisions with reason codes

components.name: TerminalGateway
components.summary: HTTPS/WSS gateway for browser-based terminal access.
responsibilities: Authenticate WebSocket or streaming connections
Validate session attachment against TerminalPolicyService
Forward terminal input/output without exposing raw tmux sockets
Disconnect expired, unauthorized or idle sessions
Sanitize output for browser rendering

components.name: OidcAuthProvider
components.summary: OIDC provider integration that maps external identity claims into Ananta's internal user, role and policy context.
responsibilities: Support Authorization Code Flow with PKCE for browser access
Validate issuer, audience, expiry, nonce and signature
Map groups/roles/claims to Ananta roles and terminal permissions
Allow local username/password authentication for local-dev fallback where enabled
Avoid granting permissions for unknown claims or unmapped roles

terminal_targets.summary: Normal remote runtime console for a selected Worker or Worker workspace. This is the expected default target.
Direct terminal access to the Hub runtime. This is high risk and must require separate explicit permissions.
Hub process acting as Worker via HUB_CAN_BE_WORKER=true. Must be displayed and authorized separately from a normal Worker.

permissions.name: terminal.worker.create
permissions.summary: Create terminal sessions on Worker targets.

permissions.name: terminal.worker.read
permissions.summary: Read output from Worker terminal sessions.

permissions.name: terminal.worker.write
permissions.summary: Send input to Worker terminal sessions.

permissions.name: terminal.hub.list
permissions.summary: List Hub terminal-capable target. Must not imply attach/write access.

permissions.name: terminal.hub.create
permissions.summary: Create direct Hub terminal session. High risk and denied by default.

permissions.name: terminal.hub.attach
permissions.summary: Attach to direct Hub terminal session. High risk and denied by default.

permissions.name: terminal.hub.read
permissions.summary: Read output from Hub terminal session. High risk and denied by default.

permissions.name: terminal.hub.write
permissions.summary: Send input to Hub terminal session. High risk and denied by default.

permissions.name: terminal.hub_as_worker.create
permissions.summary: Create terminal session for Hub when Hub acts as Worker. Must not be treated as normal Worker access.

permissions.name: terminal.privileged_command.request
permissions.summary: Request execution of privileged command class. Does not itself allow execution.

permissions.name: terminal.secret_path.access
permissions.summary: Allow terminal session to access configured secret paths. Denied by default.

implementation_tasks.title: Expose terminal-capable targets for Hub, Workers and Hub-as-Worker
implementation_tasks.summary: Add a deterministic target inventory endpoint and internal resolver so the UI/CLI can show exactly where a terminal session can be opened.
details: Return target_type values worker, hub and hub_as_worker.
Expose Hub target only as terminal-capable when explicitly configured.
Expose Hub-as-Worker as a separate target when HUB_CAN_BE_WORKER=true.
Do not merge Hub-as-Worker into normal Worker list silently.
Include display name, runtime URL, health state and risk class.
acceptance_criteria: GET /terminal/targets returns Worker targets when Workers are registered and policy allows listing.
GET /terminal/targets can include target_type=hub only when terminal.hub.list is allowed.
GET /terminal/targets returns target_type=hub_as_worker separately when HUB_CAN_BE_WORKER=true.
A user with only terminal.worker.list does not see Hub terminal attach/write capability.
Tests cover Hub-only, Worker-only, Hub-as-Worker and mixed deployments.

implementation_tasks.title: Persist terminal session metadata and lifecycle events
implementation_tasks.summary: Introduce database models and repositories for terminal sessions and terminal audit events.
details: Add TerminalSessionDB model.
Add TerminalEventDB model.
Add repositories for session CRUD and event append/read.
Store target_type, target_id, user binding, workspace binding and policy decision id.
Keep raw terminal output out of normal audit table unless recording is explicitly enabled.
acceptance_criteria: A terminal session cannot be persisted without target_type, target_id and created_by_user_id.
A terminal session cannot be persisted without policy_decision_id after creation authorization.
Session status transitions are tested for created, running, attached, detached, killed and expired.
Terminal events are append-only through repository APIs.
Repository tests pass on the configured test database.

implementation_tasks.title: Implement safe tmux backend
implementation_tasks.summary: Create a tmux backend that can start, attach, send input, capture output and kill sessions without shell injection or unsafe target names.
details: Use subprocess argument arrays instead of shell=True.
Generate tmux session names from safe prefixes and UUIDs.
Reject user-provided tmux names unless normalized into a safe internal id.
Support read-only capture mode.
Support cleanup for stale sessions.
Return structured errors rather than raw tmux stderr where possible.
acceptance_criteria: Session names containing shell metacharacters are rejected or normalized safely.
No tmux command path uses shell=True for untrusted input.
Creating a session returns a structured backend session object.
Input is sent only to the expected tmux session and pane.
Killing a session affects only the expected tmux session.
Tests cover missing tmux binary with a clear error message.

implementation_tasks.title: Add terminal-specific authorization checks
implementation_tasks.summary: Extend Ananta authorization with deterministic terminal permissions and reason-coded policy decisions.
details: Add operation-level checks for list, create, attach, read, write and kill.
Separate terminal.worker.*, terminal.hub.* and terminal.hub_as_worker.* permissions.
Make Hub terminal access deny-by-default even for users who can access Worker terminals.
Return decision object with allow boolean and reason code.
Ensure admin role does not bypass terminal policy unless explicitly configured.
acceptance_criteria: terminal.worker.attach does not imply terminal.hub.attach.
terminal.worker.write does not imply terminal.hub.write.
Hub terminal creation is denied by default.
Hub-as-Worker terminal creation requires terminal.hub_as_worker.create.
Every denied decision includes a stable reason code.
Every allowed decision includes policy version and matched rule id.

implementation_tasks.title: Add REST API for terminal sessions
implementation_tasks.summary: Expose controlled terminal management endpoints through the Hub API using existing Bearer token authentication.
details: Add routes for listing targets and sessions.
Add route for creating sessions with target_type, target_id, workspace_path, goal_id and task_id.
Add route for reading terminal output.
Add route for sending input with write permission checks.
Add route for killing a session.
All routes must use existing auth context and TerminalPolicyService.
acceptance_criteria: Unauthenticated requests receive 401.
Authenticated but unauthorized terminal requests receive 403 with stable reason code.
POST /terminal/sessions refuses missing target_type and target_id.
POST /terminal/sessions refuses Hub target without terminal.hub.create.
POST /terminal/sessions/{id}/input refuses write input for read-only sessions.
DELETE /terminal/sessions/{id} emits an audit event.

implementation_tasks.title: Add ananta tmux CLI commands
implementation_tasks.summary: Add CLI commands for listing targets, starting sessions, attaching, listing sessions and killing sessions.
details: Add ananta tmux targets.
Add ananta tmux start.
Add ananta tmux attach.
Add ananta tmux list.
Add ananta tmux kill.
Use existing ANANTA_BASE_URL authentication behavior.
Show clear warnings for target_type=hub and target_type=hub_as_worker.
acceptance_criteria: ananta tmux targets prints target type, id, display name and risk class.
ananta tmux start defaults to Worker target when only one eligible Worker exists.
ananta tmux start --target-type hub requires explicit --reason.
CLI refuses ambiguous target selection instead of guessing.
CLI shows authorization failures without leaking tokens or stack traces.
CLI documentation includes local and remote examples.

implementation_tasks.title: Integrate terminal sessions into Ananta TUI
implementation_tasks.summary: Add a TUI screen for controlled terminal targets and sessions.
details: Add target list view.
Add active session list view.
Add attach/detach/kill actions.
Display target risk class clearly.
Mark Hub and Hub-as-Worker targets as high risk.
Sanitize terminal output before rendering in TUI.
acceptance_criteria: ananta tui --tmux opens terminal session overview.
Hub target is visually distinct from Worker target.
Read-only sessions cannot send input from TUI.
TUI handles lost Hub connection gracefully.
TUI does not render unsafe control sequences without sanitization.

implementation_tasks.title: Add HTTPS/WSS terminal gateway
implementation_tasks.summary: Expose browser terminal access over HTTPS/WSS without exposing tmux sockets or direct shell ports.
details: Add short-lived attach token endpoint.
Validate attach token and user session before WebSocket upgrade.
Disconnect when token expires or policy is revoked.
Support read-only stream mode and interactive mode.
Sanitize terminal output before browser rendering.
Do not expose tmux socket path to frontend.
acceptance_criteria: WSS connection without valid token is rejected.
Expired attach token cannot open a stream.
Attach token is scoped to exactly one session and one user.
User cannot connect to another user's session by guessing session_id.
Browser terminal works over HTTPS/WSS only in production profile.
Gateway disconnect event is audit logged.

implementation_tasks.title: Add OIDC provider integration for web terminal access
implementation_tasks.summary: Add OIDC login support that maps external identity into Ananta's internal auth and authorization context.
details: Support Authorization Code Flow with PKCE.
Validate OIDC issuer, audience, expiry, nonce and signature.
Map groups/roles/claims to internal Ananta roles and terminal permissions.
Support production config that disables local username/password login.
Keep local login available for local-dev when explicitly enabled.
Document Keycloak example configuration.
acceptance_criteria: OIDC login creates valid Ananta user auth context.
Unknown OIDC group does not grant terminal permission.
OIDC role mapping is deterministic and tested.
Invalid issuer, audience or signature is rejected.
Local login can be disabled by configuration in production profile.
OIDC-authenticated user is subject to the same TerminalPolicyService decisions as local users.

implementation_tasks.title: Add terminal timeout, idle cleanup and lifecycle enforcement
implementation_tasks.summary: Prevent abandoned or long-running terminal sessions from becoming permanent uncontrolled access paths.
details: Add max idle timeout.
Add max lifetime timeout.
Add cleanup tick for expired sessions.
Detach WebSocket clients when session expires.
Kill or detach tmux session depending on policy.
acceptance_criteria: Idle sessions expire after configured idle timeout.
Sessions cannot exceed configured max lifetime.
Expired session cannot receive input.
Cleanup emits terminal_session_expired audit event.
Cleanup does not kill unrelated tmux sessions.

implementation_tasks.title: Add optional terminal recording with redaction
implementation_tasks.summary: Record terminal events and optionally terminal output while redacting known secret patterns.
details: Record lifecycle events by default.
Make full output recording configurable by policy.
Redact common token, key and password patterns.
Avoid logging raw input when configured as sensitive.
Store recording metadata separate from normal audit event summary.
acceptance_criteria: Lifecycle audit events are always emitted for remote sessions.
Full output recording can be disabled for local-dev.
Secret-like values are redacted in recorded output where detectable.
Raw Bearer tokens are never stored in audit events.
Recording policy is visible in session metadata.

implementation_tasks.title: Add container and compose support for tmux terminal capability
implementation_tasks.summary: Ensure tmux and terminal gateway work in Docker profiles without widening host access accidentally.
details: Install tmux where terminal capability is enabled.
Add config flags for enabling/disabling terminal feature.
Do not mount host root or Docker socket by default.
Document local-dev vs production settings.
Keep terminal feature disabled unless explicitly configured for production.
acceptance_criteria: Docker profile starts without tmux feature when disabled.
Docker profile with terminal enabled has tmux available.
No default compose file exposes a raw terminal port.
No default compose file mounts / or /var/run/docker.sock for terminal use.
Documentation shows safe local-dev setup.

implementation_tasks.title: Add Angular frontend terminal UI
implementation_tasks.summary: Add browser UI for listing targets, creating sessions and connecting via secure terminal gateway.
details: Show targets grouped by Worker, Hub and Hub-as-Worker.
Show high-risk warning for Hub and Hub-as-Worker.
Require explicit user action before opening Hub terminal.
Use attach token flow for WSS connection.
Render read-only and interactive sessions differently.
Show policy-denied reason messages without leaking internals.
acceptance_criteria: User can list terminal-capable targets from UI.
Hub target is not displayed as normal Worker.
Opening Hub terminal requires explicit confirmation and policy permission.
Unauthorized target actions are disabled or return clear 403 message.
WSS connection uses scoped attach token, not long-lived raw JWT in URL where avoidable.
UI survives gateway disconnect with clear status.

implementation_tasks.title: Document terminal threat model, OIDC setup and operational rules
implementation_tasks.summary: Add documentation that makes the security model explicit and prevents accidental insecure deployment.
details: Document terminal risk classes.
Document why Hub terminal access is denied by default.
Document Hub vs Worker vs Hub-as-Worker distinction.
Document Keycloak/OIDC example config.
Document local-dev fallback behavior.
Document safe reverse proxy settings for HTTPS/WSS.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include explicit warning against raw tmux socket exposure.
Docs include permission matrix for worker, hub and hub_as_worker.
Docs include local CLI/TUI examples.
Docs include production OIDC examples.
Docs explain that admin does not automatically bypass terminal policy.

global_acceptance_criteria: Terminal sessions are unavailable unless the feature is explicitly enabled or local-dev mode allows local tmux usage.
No unauthenticated terminal access exists through REST, WebSocket, CLI or UI.
Worker terminal permission does not grant Hub terminal permission.
Hub-as-Worker is represented as its own target type and permission class.
All terminal create, attach, write and kill operations call TerminalPolicyService.
All remote terminal operations emit audit events with user, target, session and policy decision metadata.
Raw tmux sockets are never exposed over the network.
HTTPS/WSS terminal access uses short-lived scoped attach tokens or equivalent session-bound authorization.
OIDC users are mapped deterministically to Ananta roles and policies.
Unknown OIDC claims never grant terminal permissions by default.
Local username/password login can be disabled for production deployments.
Terminal output and input handling avoids leaking Bearer tokens and known secrets in logs.

unit_tests: OIDC claim mapping and rejection paths
integration_tests: Create Worker terminal session through REST API
Create Hub-as-Worker session only with explicit permission
Attach browser gateway with scoped token
Expire idle session and reject further input
manual_smoke_tests: ananta tmux start --target-type hub --reason local-admin-debug with permission
Browser terminal via HTTPS/WSS with OIDC login
security_tests: Attempt shell injection through session name
Attempt path traversal through workspace path
Attempt attach to another user's session
Attempt Worker permission reuse for Hub terminal
Attempt direct raw tmux socket access

open_questions: Should Hub terminal access require interactive approval even when policy allows it?
Should production Hub terminal sessions be read-only by default?
Should terminal output recording be mandatory for Hub sessions?
Should privileged commands be blocked by pattern, shell wrapper, container isolation or all three?
Should terminal sessions be attached to Goals/Tasks only, or also support standalone maintenance sessions?

summaries.T001: TerminalTargetService exposes worker/hub/hub_as_worker targets controlled by TERMINAL_*_ENABLED config flags. Hub-as-Worker is always a separate target type. Tests in test_terminal_targets.py.
summaries.T002: TerminalSessionDB + TerminalEventDB models in db_models.py. TerminalSessionRepository and TerminalEventRepository in agent/repositories/terminal.py. Repository validates target_type, target_id, user and policy_decision_id on every save. Tests in test_terminal_session_repository.py.
summaries.T003: TmuxSessionBackend in agent/services/tmux_backend.py. Uses subprocess argument arrays only (no shell=True). Session names generated from safe prefix + UUID, validated against strict regex. create_session/send_input/capture_output/kill_session all in place.
summaries.T004: TerminalPolicyService in agent/services/terminal_policy_service.py. Separate terminal.worker.* / terminal.hub.* / terminal.hub_as_worker.* permission namespaces. Hub terminal create/attach/read/write denied by default even for admin. Every decision returns allow + reason_code + decision_id + policy_version. Tests in test_terminal_policy_service.py.
summaries.T005: Full REST API in agent/routes/terminal.py: GET /targets, GET/POST /sessions, GET /sessions/{id}, POST /sessions/{id}/input, GET /sessions/{id}/output, POST /sessions/{id}/attach-token, DELETE /sessions/{id}. All routes enforce policy checks and emit audit events. Tests in test_terminal_routes.py.
summaries.T006: ananta tmux targets/start/attach/list/kill commands in agent/cli/commands/tmux.py. New agent/cli/api_client.py provides thin HTTP wrapper reusing ANANTA_BASE_URL/ANANTA_USER/ANANTA_PASSWORD. Hub/hub_as_worker start requires --reason flag and shows HIGH RISK warning. Ambiguous multi-worker target selection refused.
summaries.T007: 'terminal' section added to SECTIONS in client_surfaces/operator_tui/sections.py (alias 'term'). _terminal_content_lines() in renderer.py shows target list with HIGH RISK markers, active session list, and command hints.
summaries.T008: New /ws/terminal/session WSS endpoint in agent/ws_terminal.py. _attach_token_ws_session() validates single-use scoped attach token, bridges tmux output via capture_output polling, forwards input via send_input, redacts secrets in output, enforces idle/max-lifetime timeouts, emits session_attached and gateway_disconnected audit events, transitions session to detached on close. docs/security/terminal-gateway.md added. Tests in test_terminal_gateway_auth.py.
summaries.T009: OIDC Authorization Code Flow with PKCE in agent/routes/auth_oidc.py. Routes: GET /auth/oidc/login, GET /auth/oidc/callback, GET /auth/oidc/userinfo, POST /auth/oidc/logout. Validates issuer/audience/expiry/nonce/signature via PyJWT. Maps groups (ananta-admin, ananta-user, ananta-viewer, ananta-terminal-hub, ananta-terminal-worker) to Ananta roles and terminal permissions. 9 new config fields in agent/config.py. docs/security/oidc.md with Keycloak example.
summaries.T010: TerminalCleanupService in agent/services/terminal_cleanup_service.py. run_cleanup_tick() iterates all active sessions and expires those exceeding idle_expires_at or expires_at. Kills tmux session, transitions DB status to 'expired', emits terminal_session_expired audit event. TERMINAL_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (900s) and TERMINAL_MAX_LIFETIME_SECONDS (14400s) configurable. Expired sessions reject further input. Tests in test_terminal_session_cleanup.py.
summaries.T011: TerminalRecordingService in agent/services/terminal_recording_service.py. redact_secrets() strips Bearer tokens, passwords, API keys, ANANTA_TOKEN/ANANTA_PASSWORD env vars, AWS-style access keys, private key blocks. Called on every output chunk in REST output endpoint and WSS gateway. recording_enabled flag on session. 9 redaction patterns, 9 tests in test_terminal_recording_redaction.py.
summaries.T012: tmux installed in Dockerfile and Dockerfile.quickstart-no-ollama apt-get install blocks. TERMINAL_FEATURE_ENABLED, TERMINAL_WORKER/HUB/HUB_AS_WORKER_TARGET_ENABLED, TERMINAL_IDLE/MAX_LIFETIME/ATTACH_TOKEN config vars, TERMINAL_RECORDING_ENABLED_REMOTE/LOCAL_DEV, TERMINAL_OIDC_ENABLED all added to x-agent-env in docker-compose.base.yml. All default to safe values (feature disabled, hub disabled, recording enabled for remote).
summaries.T013: TerminalApiService in frontend-angular/src/app/services/terminal-api.service.ts: listTargets, listSessions, createSession, getSession, getAttachToken, killSession, toWssUrl. TerminalTargetsComponent in terminal-targets.component.ts: groups targets, shows HIGH RISK badge, requires explicit confirmation before opening Hub/Hub-as-Worker terminal, emits attach token on create. TerminalSessionListComponent in terminal-session-list.component.ts: table view with status badges, type badges (hub/worker color-coded), attach and kill actions. terminal.service.ts extended with connectWithAttachToken() for WSS attach-token flow.
summaries.T014: docs/security/terminal-threat-model.md: target classification, permission matrix, hard denies, attach token security, session lifecycle, why admin does not bypass terminal policy, audit event table, container notes, OIDC security notes. docs/security/terminal-gateway.md: WSS flow diagram, attach token properties, server/client message types, lifecycle, production nginx config. docs/security/oidc.md: OIDC flow, claim mapping table, Keycloak setup example, local dev fallback.
